title: Nervilia macroglossa
taxonomy: Nervilia macroglossa is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants with over 30,000 known species. The genus Nervilia itself is a small group of orchids, comprising around 20 species. It is a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on land, and is often found in shaded and humid environments.

intro: Nervilia macroglossa, also known as the tongue orchid, is a type of orchid that belongs to the genus Nervilia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cold, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Nervilia macroglossa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nervilia macroglossa is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory conditions.
